title: Crumbly Chinese Bitter Melon Crumble
description: A romantic, bittersweet crumble that blends the unique flavor of bitter melon with a golden, crumbly topping, all while staying eggfree and comforting.

ingredients.name: bitter melon
ingredients.notes: sliced lengthwise, seeded and salted for 10 min, then drained

instructions: In a skillet, heat 1 Tbsp of oil over medium heat. Add the sliced bitter melon, sprinkle with a pinch of salt, and sauté for 5–7 min until softened and lightly browned. Remove from heat and set aside. In the same skillet, add the remaining oil, then stir in the sugar and 1/2 cup of evaporated milk. Cook until the mixture thickens slightly, stirring constantly, about 3–4 min; remove from heat.

In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt. Stir in chopped nuts and sesame seeds. Cut in the cold butter cubes using a pastry cutter or your fingers until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gently fold in the sweetened evaporated milk mixture, then the sautéed bitter melon, mixing until just combined. Pour the mixture into a 9 × 13 in baking dish. Sprinkle the top with a thin layer of the remaining butter cubes for a crisp finish.
Bake for 25–30 min, or until the top is golden brown and the crumble is set. Let it cool for 10 min before serving.
Serve warm, optionally drizzled with a light soy sauce glaze or a sprinkle of fresh cilantro for an extra touch of romance.
